svg.text: AI-native hybrid database in Rust · One binary serves REST, gRPC, pgwire over a shared corpus with HNSW + RAG · Leader/follower replication · Multi-region HA

g.text: nebula-server · axum router · request middleware chain
Outer → inner: rate-limit → audit → follower-write guard → auth → handler. Ops endpoints (/healthz, /metrics) bypass auth + limit so scrapes survive abuse.
Query planes · how a request becomes work
4. Residual filter / GROUP BY / JOIN in-memory
5. Shape rows for REST · gRPC · pgwire
Core engines · stateless logic, swappable trait objects
Every write is fsynced to the WAL before the index sees it. Recovery replays from the latest snapshot forward.
Leader / follower (declarative · NEBULA_NODE_ROLE). Single writer, N readers. Followers tail WAL via gRPC stream.
• Cursor durable → resume from last applied LSN, no re-stream
• DNS / LB swings reads → nearest healthy follower (multi-region)
• Embedding cache: hot keys promote L1
• Read traffic steered to healthy followers
• Tokio async; one runtime serves all 3 protocols
• L1 embedding cache → memcpy hit, no RPC
• Stateless edge — N replicas behind LB
• Redis layer optional · cache outage transparent
• Graceful degrade: Mock LLM if upstream down
